description:
[octopus] When ProjectServer broadcasts {:project, :tickets_changed}, every connected LiveView calls back into ProjectServer synchronously (get_children, get_ticket, subscribe). If ProjectServer is still processing the ticket refresh that triggered the broadcast, these calls timeout at 5s and crash the LiveView processes.

Particularly bad when deleting a worktree — the cascade crashes the entire UI and triggers repeated mount failures.

## Root cause

WorkspaceLive.refresh_selected_ticket/1 does GenServer.call to ProjectServer for get_children and get_ticket inside handle_info for tickets_changed. N LiveViews × M calls = ProjectServer mailbox flood while it's still busy.

## Fix options

1. Include ticket/children data in the broadcast payload so LiveViews don't need to call back
2. Make the calls async (cast + send_update pattern)
3. Cache recent ticket data in an ETS table that LiveViews read directly

## Relevant code

- lib/octopus_web/live/workspace_live.ex:915 (handle_info for tickets_changed)
- lib/octopus_web/live/workspace_live.ex:1237-1238 (refresh_selected_ticket calls get_ticket + get_children)

Note: filed here because bw can't open the octopus repo (bw-pqi).
title: Fix tickets_changed callback cascade crashing ProjectServer
